danger_signals.meaning: Player has been hit by power-up attack or collision; loss of control and momentum

danger_signals.visual_form: Coins scatter from kart or coin counter decreases
danger_signals.meaning: Damage taken; reduced speed advantage; vulnerability increases
Risk of respawn penalty; immediate speed reduction; loss of coins

danger_signals.visual_form: Shell or banana visual approaching on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Attack incoming; need evasion or invincibility power-up
Vulnerable state; next hit will cause spin-out instead of coin loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Kart surrounded by star aura or visual effect
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invincibility; can ram through attacks without consequence

safety_signals.visual_form: Coin counter displays value; coins visible on kart
safety_signals.meaning: Damage buffer; protection against next single attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Chevron-marked panels on track with directional visual feedback

safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity for air maneuvers; speed maintenance during turn

safety_signals.visual_form: Traffic light turns green via Lakitu's pole
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to accelerate; race officially begun

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mushroom Cup tracks feature Mario-themed environments

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Computer-controlled Bowser kart has distinct visual appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Special Cup likely features darker, castle-themed track variants
Flower Cup tracks feature royal/garden-themed environments

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Computer-controlled Luigi kart distinguishable from Mario
Computer-controlled Donkey Kong kart visually heavy/distinct

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Green traffic light from Lakitu; countdown timer visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Karts accelerate from starting line; AI racers engage in competitive racing behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rear-view mirror shows no opponents immediately behind; position indicator shows first place
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can focus on optimal line; less frequent attack power-up usage by AI

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponents visible ahead in main viewport; mini-map shows distance gap
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI racers employ offensive power-ups more frequently; player has increased attack opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Balloons visible on karts; different track layout without racing finish line
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Karts prioritize attacks over speed; collision/combat focused movement patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer prominently displayed; no opponent karts visible; ghost data may appear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single-player racing against clock; no AI interference or power-up attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI labels show difficulty; track speed perception increases; AI kart behavior more aggressive
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI racers use power-ups more strategically; tighter racing lines; more frequent attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New cups unlock visually in menu; Special Cup becomes available after 100cc wins
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to higher difficulty tracks; progression to more challenging AI opponents

environmental_storytelling.example: Mushroom Cup features mushroom-motif tracks; Flower Cup features garden/floral environments; Star Cup escalates visual complexity
Boost panels and ramps are integrated Mario-universe mechanics; their placement communicates safe vs. risky routing
Each racer's kart directly reflects their character design; Mario's red-blue kart, Bowser's dark kart, Peach's pink kart communicate faction identity at a glance
Bottom-screen map shows relative positions of all racers; visual spread indicates catch-up opportunity or lead security
Lines of coins placed strategically on tracks; their location suggests optimal vs. risky paths; collecting indicates smart racing decisions

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Uses offensive power-ups (shells, bananas) more frequently to catch up; takes aggressive line choices
Prioritizes speed and positioning; fewer attack power-ups deployed; focuses on maintaining lead
Mixed strategy; opportunistic power-up usage; reactive to nearby threats
Kart spins/loses control; temporary disadvantage; recovery attempted via power-up collection
Appears at starting line with traffic light; visual countdown signal; initiates race with green light
Can collide with objects and attacks without penalty; visual star aura indicates state; temporary dominance posture
Next hit causes immediate spin-out; more cautious path selection implied; higher defensive positioning

notes: Super Mario Kart uses minimal but effective visual language to communicate game-state and danger. The core manifestation layer relies on: (1) character/kart color identity for faction signaling, (2) coin counter as damage/vulnerability buffer, (3) power-up visual effects (star aura) for invincibility states, (4) traffic light for race start permission, (5) mini-map for positional world-state, and (6) spin-out animation for damage feedback. The game does not implement complex political factions—instead, it uses character-based identity markers to differentiate AI opponents. Danger is communicated through immediate feedback (spin-out, coin loss) rather than environmental threat signals. The paradigm is competitive racing, so 'danger' primarily means 'disadvantage in rank' rather than environmental hazard.
